title: Linked target capture
abstract: The invention generally relates to sequencing library preparation methods. In certain embodiments, two or more template nucleic acids are joined together by a linking molecule, such as a PEG derivative. Identical copies of a nucleic acid fragment or both strands of a duplex fragment may be linked together. The linked nucleic acids are amplified, creating linked amplicons. Emulsion PCR with linked primers creates linked template nucleic acids for seeding sequencing clusters and errors can be readily identified by their presence on only one of the linked fragments.
claims: 1. A method for sequencing a region of interest, the method comprising: providing adapters wherein each adapter comprises at least a double stranded stem, wherein a first strand of the stem comprises a universal priming site and a barcode that is 3′ of the universal priming site within the first strand and wherein a second strand of the stem comprises a reverse complement of the universal priming site and a reverse complement of the barcode; ligating the adapters onto a plurality of duplex nucleic acid fragments wherein the plurality of duplex nucleic acid fragments comprise a sense strand and an antisense strand; denaturing the plurality of ligated duplex nucleic acid fragments to create single-stranded nucleic acid fragments each comprising one of the universal priming site or the reverse complement of the universal priming site; amplifying the single-stranded nucleic acid fragments using universal primers physically linked to a probe that anneals to a target within the sense strand or a complement of the target to generate a plurality of amplified fragments comprising copies of the sense and antisense strands labelled with the barcode or the reverse complement of the barcode; and sequencing the plurality of amplified fragments to determine a sequence of the region of interest.
